title: Polyscias pleiosperma
taxonomy: Polyscias pleiosperma is classified within the family Araliaceae. This family comprises a group of flowering plants commonly known as ivy palms or salsify. The genus Polyscias is a part of the Araliaceae family and consists of tropical and subtropical plants. Polyscias pleiosperma is native to tropical Africa and is a member of the Araliaceae family, which includes around 50-60 genera. The classification of Polyscias pleiosperma is based on its morphological characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Araliaceae family.
uses: Polyscias pleiosperma is native to the Pacific Islands, and while information is limited, it has been reported to have potential medicinal uses.
